findings.evidence: New code defines `driftForm` with `LinearMap.mkContinuous₂` at lines 45-60; Mathlib has `innerSL` and `innerSL_apply_apply` at `Mathlib/Analysis/InnerProductSpace/LinearMap.lean:164-172`, and `smulRightL` with `smulRightL (c) (f) (x) = c x • f` at `Mathlib/Analysis/Normed/Operator/Bilinear.lean:412-418`, plus `norm_smulRightL` at `Mathlib/Analysis/Normed/Operator/NormedSpace.lean:214`.
findings.fix: Replace the custom `mkContinuous₂` definition and norm lemmas by this existing `smulRightL`/`innerSL` construction and its norm API.
findings.issue: `driftForm` reinvents Mathlib's rank-one continuous map: it is `ContinuousLinearMap.smulRightL ℝ (EuclideanSpace ℝ n) ℝ (innerSL ℝ b)`, up to commutativity of real multiplication.

findings.evidence: New code defines `massForm` by `LinearMap.mkContinuous₂` at lines 105-114; Mathlib already provides `ContinuousLinearMap.mul : R →L[𝕜] R →L[𝕜] R` and `mul_apply'` at `Mathlib/Analysis/Normed/Operator/Mul.lean:40-45`, with `opNorm_mul_le` at line 52.
findings.fix: Remove `massForm` or define it as the existing `c • ContinuousLinearMap.mul ℝ ℝ` and reuse `ContinuousLinearMap.mul_apply'`, `opNorm_mul_le`, and scalar-norm lemmas instead of reproving the API.
findings.issue: `massForm c` is just scalar multiplication of Mathlib's bundled continuous multiplication, `c • ContinuousLinearMap.mul ℝ ℝ`, so the definition and its basic apply/add/smul/norm API are parallel duplicates.

summary: The new pointwise forms duplicate existing bundled continuous bilinear/rank-one maps from Mathlib instead of reusing them.
